t_ch: 佩拉是我的好朋友。我们在贝洛伯格士官学校读书的时候就认识了。
t_en: She's a good friend of mine. We go way back to our days at the Belobog Cadet School.

t_ch: 我们第一次认识是在学校的图书馆里。那时，我捡到了她丢掉的创作——和《奇谭》有关的那种——还给她了。
t_en: The first time we met was at the school library. At the time, I happened to pick up her unfinished writing, something like Tales, and returned it to her.

t_ch: 我没想到学校里竟然还有第二个《奇谭》粉丝，就想和她聊聊，听听她对《奇谭》的看法…不过，那时的佩拉很害羞，支支吾吾什么也没说出来。
t_en: I was pretty surprised to find out that she was a fan of Tales too, so I was curious about her opinion on it... But Pela was so shy that she didn't really say anything.

t_ch: 至于后来…我们俩再见面的时候，就是在姐姐的乐队里了。
t_en: Later on... we met again in Serval's band.

t_ch: 听说你也是乐队成员…
t_en: You're a member of the band, right?

t_ch: 嗯，我是键盘手。
t_en: Yeah, I'm the keyboard player.

t_ch: 「机械热潮」的前一任键盘手是邓恩大哥。他毕业以后军务缠身，就离开了乐队——而我补上了键盘手的位置。
t_en: Dunn was the keyboard player for Mechanical Fever before I joined, but he got caught up in military duties after graduation and had to leave the band, so I filled in.

t_ch: 不过，听说在我加入乐队以前，乐队应该还有一个人——那人好像是贝斯手，后来失踪了，据说和姐姐、还有可可利亚大人有些联系……
t_en: But from what I heard, there was actually another member before me, a bass player I think? But then they just disappeared, and I heard they had some connection with my sister and Madam Cocolia...

t_ch: …不过这也是我道听途说来的，不是很清楚其中的细节。你就当是个幻想故事吧，别放在心上。
t_en: ...But these are just baseless rumors, and I don't really know the details. So, don't take this wild story too seriously.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 唔，好。如果还有想问的，可以随时问我。
t_en: Hmm, yeah. Feel free to come to me if you have any more questions.

t_ch: 不打扰你科考了。
t_en: Then I'll leave you to your research.

t_ch: 路上小心，{NICKNAME}。愿你的旅途里都是晴天。
t_en: So long, {NICKNAME}. I wish you a smooth ride ahead.

t_ch: 唷，{NICKNAME}！你居然还在下层区，真是好兴致啊！
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}! Glad to see you here, in the Underworld!

t_ch: 怎么样，要不要上台切磋切磋？
t_en: How about a friendly spar! What do you say?

t_ch: 没出场费的比赛我不打。
t_en: I don't fight for free.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，不愧是你！早听人说贝洛伯格的大英雄惜财如命，看来关于你的传言不都是毫无根据嘛！
t_en: Hahahaha, I knew you'd say that! Rumor has it that the great hero of Belobog is stingy! Looks like those rumors about you are true after all!

t_ch: 我可不想欺负伤员。
t_en: I have no interest in bullying someone who's injured.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，你可别小看我！区区小伤，我躺上几分钟就恢复如初了！
t_en: Hahahaha, they're just some scratches! I'll be alive and kicking again in a few minutes!

t_ch: 看样子，你现在不在切磋的兴头上啊…可惜，那就下次吧！下次我可不想再听借口了哦！
t_en: Such a shame that you're not in the mood for a spar... Fine, let's fight next time! No more excuses, hah!

t_ch: 唷，{NICKNAME}！你居然还在下层区，真是好兴致啊！
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}! Glad to see you here, in the Underworld!

t_ch: 怎么，想动动筋骨，稍微出两滴汗？
t_en: Hmm? Want to get some exercise and break a sweat?

t_ch: 我们可以聊聊天。
t_en: We can have a chat.

t_ch: 聊天？行啊，当然可以——毕竟跟大英雄谈心的机会可不是每天都有。
t_en: Chat? Sure thing! You don't get to chat with a great hero every day!

t_ch: 你是<unbreak>28</unbreak>届搏击冠军？
t_en: Are you the champion of the <unbreak>28</unbreak>-time fighting champion?

t_ch: 嗯？哈哈哈，没想到连次数你都记得这么清楚！
t_en: Huh? Hahaha! I didn't think you'd remember the number so well!

t_ch: 我觉得这算不上什么，跟你们为贝洛伯格做的事比起来，实在不值一提！
t_en: Ah, don't mention it! It's nothing compared to what you've done for Belobog!

t_ch: 我不是为了争夺什么荣誉才参加搏击比赛的…嗐，听着是不是有点故作谦虚的意思？但我真的是这么想的，你得信我！
t_en: To be honest, I didn't join the tournament for fame or recognition... Sounds a bit deliberately modest, huh? But trust me, I mean it!

t_ch: 你为史考特打拳吗？
t_en: Are you fighting for Scott?

t_ch: 史考特？
t_en: Scott?

t_ch: 哦…你说的是俱乐部的老板史考特先生吧？没有，我跟他不熟。
t_en: Oh... You mean the boss of the club? No, I don't really know him well.

t_ch: 老实说，我不太喜欢他搞的那套规矩，拳手经纪人什么的…我觉得没必要，太浮夸了。我只想靠自己的实力说话，其它的一概不感兴趣。
t_en: To be frank, I'm not a fan of his rules, especially the ones about agents and all that... I prefer to focus on my own strength and abilities and not worry about all the fancy stuff.

t_ch: 最近「地火」在忙什么呢？
t_en: What's Wildfire been up to lately?

t_ch: 多亏了你们，我们最近的活计都轻松了不少。
t_en: Thanks to you guys, our work has been a lot easier lately.

t_ch: 日常的放哨巡逻当然不能落下，偶尔还要帮街里街坊的解决些小矛盾…哦，对了，最近还要配合铁卫搞几场联合演练，这事不能忘。
t_en: Of course, we don't slack off on our daily patrols, and sometimes we help the neighborhood resolve conflicts... Oh, and we need to hold some joint exercises with the Silvermane Guards soon. I mustn't forget about that.

t_ch: 你的「试炼」很特别。
t_en: Your "trial" was special.

t_ch: 啊，你是说我跟史瓦罗大佬的那场比赛？
t_en: Ah, you mean the match between me and Svarog?

t_ch: 的确很特别！对我来说，那场比试有很特殊的意义。
t_en: It was special indeed! That match had a lot of meaning to me.

t_ch: 我最大的遗憾就是没能赢下来…至少打个平手也行。毕竟我答应过玛……
t_en: It's such a shame that I didn't win the fight... or at least tied. I promised Margie that...

t_ch: …啊，没什么！史瓦罗大佬果然名不虚传，要打败他这个地底最强，果然我还是得勤加锻炼才行！
t_en: ...Ah, never mind! Svarog is indeed an awesome fighter! I guess I'll have to work harder if I want to best him, the strongest fighter!

t_ch: 回见，卢卡。
t_en: Until next time, Luka.

t_ch: 回见，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Yeah, see you, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 哟，这不是{NICKNAME}吗？真是好久不见了。
t_en: Hey, if it isn't {NICKNAME}! It's been a while.

t_ch: 费斯曼先生也来帮忙了啊。
t_en: Are you here to help, too, Mr. Fersman?

t_ch: 哈哈，造物之柱毕竟是关乎整个贝洛伯格的大工程，我怎么可能闲得住呢？
t_en: Haha, as you see, the Pillars of Creation is a big project that concerns all of Belobog. How can I just sit around doing nothing?

t_ch: 别看我身子骨不硬朗，但好歹也在矿场摸爬滚打了几十年，指导指导年轻一辈还是够用的。
t_en: While I may not be as tough as I was in my younger days, I've spent decades in the mines, and I'm more than capable of guiding the young'uns.

t_ch: 你好啊{NICKNAME}，虎克这孩子承蒙你照顾了。
t_en: Hey there, {NICKNAME}. Thank you for taking care of Hook.

t_ch: 有些事情想问。
t_en: I have a few questions for you.

t_ch: 「矿脉探测器」怎么样了？
t_en: Any updates about the "vein detector"?

t_ch: 哦，你说上次的事。
t_en: Oh, you mean last time, right?

t_ch: 之前巴列维过来找到我，说要买下我的「地髓矿脉探针」。
t_en: Balaway came to me and said he wanted to buy my Geomarrow Probe.

t_ch: 但我想这毕竟是虎克的心意，就没有卖给他。
t_en: But that was a gift from Hook, so I didn't sell it to him.

t_ch: 何况现在星球已经彻底开放，下层区不再需要靠争夺矿脉过活了。
t_en: Moreover, with this planet now fully accessible, Underworlders no longer need to fight over veins.

t_ch: 那台探测器本来就是古代遗物，还是把它留给过去吧。
t_en: That detector was an ancient relic to begin with, so let's just leave the past in the past.

t_ch: 造物之柱的工程进展...
t_en: How is the Pillars of Creation Project going...?

t_ch: 怎么说呢...
t_en: How should I put it...

t_ch: 这台机器这么巨大…而且构造这么复杂…就算大家拼了命地干，也不是一天两天就能把它修好的。
t_en: As you see, this machine is massive... and its composition is complicated... Even with our best efforts, there's no way we can fix it in just a day or two.

t_ch: 何况现在的这股冲劲未来能维持多久，还真不太好说。
t_en: Plus, who knows how long we can keep up this momentum?

t_ch: 不过，至少我这把老骨头又能派上一份用场，这就够了。能为这桩事业贡献一份力量，我也算是不留遗憾了……
t_en: At least now this old man can do something useful again, and that's good enough for me. Now that I've contributed to this great cause, I won't have any regrets...

t_ch: 您看起来心情不错。
t_en: You look like you're in a good mood.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，之前我还以为再也不能活着见到蓝天了呢。
t_en: Hahaha, yeah! I thought I'd never see the blue sky again.

t_ch: 如今上下层区重新打通，虎克的将来也不用发愁了。
t_en: With all the channels between the Overworld and the Underworld reopened, I no longer have to worry about Hook's future.

t_ch: 我这一辈子，无非就这点盼头，现在都有了着落。这怎能叫人不开心呢？
t_en: That's all I've ever wanted in my life, and now it's all happening. How can I not be happy, right?

t_ch: 虎克最近怎么样？
t_en: How's Hook doing?

t_ch: 虎克这孩子，自打呼吸到上层区的新鲜空气后，简直比以前还要活泼了。
t_en: Well, she has been livelier than ever since she breathed in the fresh air of the Overworld.

t_ch: 这次她又摆弄起了什么「便携式通讯装置」，还把里面会动的照片给我看...年轻人接受新事物就是快啊......
t_en: Recently, she's been tinkering with a "portable communicator" or something, and she has even showed me the moving pictures inside... Young people are always quick to embrace new stuff...

t_ch: 我打算供她上贝洛伯格的公立院校，让她学习知识、开拓眼界。
t_en: I'm planning to enroll her in a public school in Belobog so that she can learn more and broaden her horizons.

t_ch: 终有一天，这孩子会离开我的保护，成为一个可靠的大人——这在以前可是不敢想的事。
t_en: One day, she'll have to leave my care and become a reliable adult... something I've never dared to imagine before.

t_ch: 也不知道她将来会选择什么道路…啊，要是能考上士官学院就好了！从那地方毕业的，各个都是人才……
t_en: I wonder what path she will take in the future... Ah, it would be great if she could make it into Cadet School! All graduates from there have promising futures...

t_ch: 保重身体。
t_en: Take care.

t_ch: 好，也祝你旅途平安。
t_en: Great, have a safe journey!

t_ch: 好，也祝你旅途平安。
t_en: Great, have a safe journey!

t_ch: 这位{F#女士}{M#先生}，你的手机也出现了卡顿、过热、运行阻塞的情况吗？不如来我这里升级一下系统吧。
t_en: Hey, {F#Miss}{M#Mister}! Are you experiencing issues with your phone such as lagging, overheating, or system-based stoppages? I can help you upgrade your system.

t_ch: 只要花上一丢丢信用点，你的手机就能脱胎换骨，操作系统将比银河中的星轨更加丝滑……
t_en: For just a tiny amount of credits, your phone will gain a new lease on life and its operating system will function smoother than the Star Rail that lines the cosmos...

t_ch: 什么升级？
t_en: What kind of upgrade are we talking about?

t_ch: 它涉及一种关乎公司机密的独家技术，这颗星球上只有我一人掌握。
t_en: Ahem, it's actually about an exclusive piece of technology from my company, and I'm the only person on this planet who can access it.

t_ch: 因为过于先进，具体细节不方便透露。
t_en: I can't reveal too much about it since it's exclusive.

t_ch: 总之，你试试就知道了，不满意不要钱。
t_en: Anyway, give it a try and see for yourself. And don't worry. I won't charge you if you're not satisfied.

t_ch: （假装不懂）星轨？
t_en: (Pretend to be clueless) Star Rail?

t_ch: 就是星穹列车行驶的轨道——本地人肯定听说过星穹列车，对吧？就是那群来去无踪的天外来客……
t_en: It's the track on which the Astral Express runs. You must have heard of them, right? Those elusive star-seeking travelers...

t_ch: 据说就是他们把「便携式通讯装置」——哦，就是手机啦——带来了这颗星球，但显然他们完全没有顾及到「售后服务」。真是不负责任。
t_en: Rumor has it that they were the ones who brought "portable communicators" — known to us as phones — to this planet. But apparently, they don't care about customer service at all. How irresponsible!

t_ch: 而我们，星际和平公司，将会收拾他们的烂摊子！
t_en: That's why we of the IPC are here to clean up their mess!

t_ch: 那就帮我升级吧。
t_en: Please help me upgrade my phone.

t_ch: 好嘞。你放心，我已经帮助过二十多个人了。
t_en: No problem. I've already helped over two dozen customers.

t_ch: 那么接下来，我将跨越整个银河，调取位于公司总部的数据注入这台终端。
t_en: Next, I'll access data from the other side of the galaxy and transfer it straight from the IPC's headquarters into your device.

t_ch: （故作卖力）嘿...呀...哈！看，完成了。
t_en: (Pretending to work hard) Hmmm... Hyaa... Hah! Look, it's done.

t_ch: 然后呢？
t_en: And then what?

t_ch: 然后？然后你只需要尽享这丝滑的操作系统就行了！
t_en: And then voilà! Now, enjoy its ultra-smooth operating system!

t_ch: 你看——这界面，这动效，这完美的手指压感和点击反馈……
t_en: Just check it out! Look at its interface and these animations, and experience this perfectly designed haptic pressure sensors and tap-feedback system...

t_ch: …咳，别介意，我之前是负责用户体验改进的，对于这些方面比较在意……
t_en: ...Ahem, sorry, I used to work in the user experience department, so I'm a bit obsessed with these details...

t_ch: 我就是星穹列车的成员。
t_en: Well, I'm from the Astral Express.
Wait, you? This... I mean... You know...

t_ch: 我可没有说你们坏话的意思，我只是...在为本地人提供更可靠的保修业务！
t_en: I didn't mean to badmouth you! I'm just... you know... just trying to provide more reliable warranty services for the locals!

t_ch: 这些本地人，弄个密码解锁都得花上半天，要是没有我这样的专业人士指导，他们早就被各种自动扣费业务给吃干抹净了……
t_en: These folks take forever to even unlock their phones. Without professional guidance like mine, they'd fall prey to all sorts of sneaky auto-pay services...

t_ch: 你还要继续留在这里吗？
t_en: Are you sticking around here?

t_ch: 总得有谁向本地人介绍用法嘛。我已经尽可能减少收费了。
t_en: Well, someone has to teach the locals how to use these phones. I've already done my best to keep the charges as low as possible.

t_ch: 多亏了我，这里的矿工已经基本掌握了日常操作。难道不是好事一桩吗？
t_en: Thanks to me, the miners here are getting the hang of it. Isn't that a good thing?

t_ch: 你好啊{F#女士}{M#先生}。如果还有和手机相关的问题，欢迎随时来找我。
t_en: Hey there, {F#Miss}{M#Mister}. If you ever have any questions about phones, feel free to drop by anytime.

t_ch: 我想跟你聊聊。
t_en: Can we talk...?

t_ch: 你是怎么解决手机问题的？
t_en: How did you solve the issues with the phones?

t_ch: 这...呃，我可以告诉你，但你不能跟其他人说。
t_en: Well... Fine, I can tell you, but this is just between us, alright?

t_ch: 你应该知道， 现在主流的移动端操作系统都是由公司研发和维护的……
t_en: As you know, all the major operating systems are developed and maintained by the IPC...

t_ch: 为了节约算力，太长时间没有更新系统版本的终端会被标记为「已弃用」，这些手机的机主将无法享受最新推出的系统功能和优化……
t_en: To save computing power, the IPC has marked devices as "discarded" if their system hasn't been updated in a long time. As a result, the owners of those devices can't enjoy the latest features or optimizations...

t_ch: 本地人觉得自己的手机卡顿、发热、出了问题，其实只是因为他们不知道怎么升级系统而已……
t_en: Locals may experience issues such as lagging or overheating on their phones, but it's simply because they don't know how to upgrade the system...

t_ch: 所以你是想靠这个敛财？
t_en: So, you're planning to make a fortune out of this?

t_ch: 哎哟，这话不能乱讲！我前面说的可是句句属实…而且就算是简单的系统升级，在这么个偏远星球也算得上独家技术了。
t_en: Hey, that's hardly the case at all! I swear I was telling you the truth... And to be honest, even a simple system upgrade can be an exclusive gimmick on a remote planet like this.

t_ch: 那什么，你可千万别跟其他人告状啊…之前在下层区闹事的小队可被托帕总监罚的不轻…何况我只是个修手机的，没做什么伤害居民的坏事啊。
t_en: Just please don't tell anyone... Director Topaz threw the book at those troublemakers in the Underworld... I'm just a phone tinkerer who hasn't done anything to put the residents in harm's way.

t_ch: ...要不，这玩意你先拿着？多一事不如少一事，咱们就当交个朋友……
t_en: ...How about you take this thing for now? The fewer complications the better. Just treat this as the beginning of a new friendship...

t_ch: 我要去跟托帕告状。
t_en: I'll report this to Topaz.

t_ch: 别别别，千万别！我们这些小职员混口饭吃不容易啊……
t_en: No, no! Please don't! I'm just a small-time employee, and all I want is to make ends meet...

t_ch: 之前在下层区闹事的小队可被托帕总监罚的不轻…何况我只是个修手机的，没做什么伤害居民的坏事啊。
t_en: Director Topaz threw the book at those troublemakers in the Underworld... I'm just a phone tinkerer who hasn't done anything to put the residents in harm's way.

t_ch: ...要不，这玩意你先拿着？多一事不如少一事，咱们就当交个朋友……
t_en: ...How about you take this thing for now? The fewer complications the better. Just treat this as the beginning of a new friendship...

t_ch: 关于公司有些疑问...
t_en: I have some questions about the IPC...

t_ch: 托帕是个怎样的人？
t_en: What do you make of Topaz?

t_ch: 托帕总监？我这种底层员工没机会跟她打交道的。
t_en: Director Topaz? Well, I'm just a low-level employee, and I don't really have a chance to talk with her.

t_ch: 不过按照大家的话说，她是个热爱工作、善于交际的人，而且还心地善良。
t_en: But according to everyone, she's sociable, loves her work, and more importantly, has a kind heart.

t_ch: 在我们部门的<unbreak>11</unbreak>位总监里，她算是最不坏的一个了。
t_en: I think she's probably the least terrible among all the eleven directors in my department.

t_ch: 有很多星球欠公司债吗？
t_en: Are there many planets indebted to IPC?

t_ch: 我这权限看不了统计数据，还真不太好说。
t_en: I can't say for sure, as I don't have access to certain statistics.

t_ch: 不过公司有个部门叫市场开拓部，是专门负责把新星球拉入银河市场的。
t_en: However, we do have a Marketing Development Department that focuses on bringing new planets into the galactic market.

t_ch: 有时这些星球会向公司借一笔起始资金，梦想着一两个琥珀纪之后能成为数一数二的贸易枢纽。
t_en: Sometimes, those planets will borrow startup funds from us, dreaming of becoming major players in the galaxy in an Amber Era or two.

t_ch: 这样自不量力的星球，最后也往往会成为我们部门的工作对象。
t_en: However, these planets bite off more than they can chew, and they often end up becoming targets for my department.

t_ch: 你工资多少？
t_en: How much do you get paid?

t_ch: 这...不好吧，这可是商业机密。
t_en: Hey, you see... it's a trade secret.

t_ch: 你说不说？
t_en: Just spill the beans!

t_ch: 这有什么可机密的？
t_en: What's so secretive about it?

t_ch: 对不起，唯独这个真的不能说！
t_en: I'm sorry, but I just can't spill the beans on that one!

t_ch: 这是我们公司内部不可逾越的高压线，只要碰了就是死...我是说，开除。
t_en: It's a totally off-limits topic within IPC, and whoever breaks the rules will be fried... I mean, fired.

t_ch: 不光是我，你问别人也只会得到一样的回答。
t_en: It's not that I'm trying to keep it from you. Go ask anyone else, and they'll give you the same answer.

t_ch: 我还有其他事要忙。
t_en: I have other matters to attend to.

t_ch: 常来光顾啊。
t_en: Drop by anytime!

t_ch: 还在为手机困扰吗？
t_en: Still troubled over your phone?

t_ch: 全面改装，包你满意。
t_en: I'll give it a full remodel. I guarantee you'll love it!

t_ch: 瞧一瞧，看一看。
t_en: Take a gander, and have a look.

t_ch: 常来光顾啊。
t_en: Drop by anytime!

t_ch: 贵安，{NICKNAME}，我是凡迪雅，大守护者的钦差特使。
t_en: Greetings, {NICKNAME}. I'm Fedora, the envoy of the Supreme Guardian.

t_ch: 这片场地现在归我负责，但我不会干涉你们举办的娱乐活动。
t_en: I'm in charge of this venue now, but don't worry, I won't interfere with your entertainment.

t_ch: 我之所以在这，只是为了提醒公司：这里依旧是贝洛伯格的领土。
t_en: The reason I'm here is to remind the IPC that this territory still belongs to Belobog.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，很高兴再见到你。要是你能马不停蹄去做自己的事，我就更高兴了。
t_en: Glad to meet you again, {NICKNAME}. I'd be even happier if you could move on from here and onto your business.

t_ch: 你这里都卖些什么？
t_en: What are you selling here?

t_ch: 怎么，看见这张桌子就以为我是卖东西的了？
t_en: Hmm? You see a desk here and think I'm running a store?

t_ch: 这是我的私人办公区，不是店面。就算你付钱，我也没有东西能卖给你。
t_en: This is my private office, not a store. Even if you pay me, I don't have anything to sell you.

t_ch: 前面这些桌椅呢？
t_en: What about these tables and chairs in the front?

t_ch: 那些是竞技场的公共设施，一开始就在这里。放心坐吧，它不收费。
t_en: They're part of the public facilities in the arena and had been since its installation. Feel free to take a break there for free.

t_ch: 后面那个牌子呢？
t_en: And the sign in the back?

t_ch: 那只是比赛的宣传画，看样子就知道了吧？
t_en: That's just a poster for the tournament. You can tell purely from its appearance, I presume?

t_ch: 可你这里真的像商店。
t_en: But this place really looks like a store.

t_ch: 好吧，为了打消你的十万个为什么，我想我还是解释一下比较好。
t_en: All right, to cease your seemingly endless confusion, please allow me to explain.

t_ch: 这里确实曾经有一个卖食物的小商贩。后来他回上层区结婚了，这张桌子和位置也就归我了。
t_en: There used to be a food vendor here, but he returned to the Overworld and got married. That's why I've taken over this table and spot.

t_ch: 明白了？我没法给你提供任何东西。
t_en: So, you understand now? There is nothing I can offer you here.

t_ch: 你应该去参加比赛或做点什么别的，而不是把功夫浪费在跟我说话上。
t_en: Your time would be more appropriately used by joining a match or something rather than talking with me.

t_ch: 你就没有什么任务交给我吗？
t_en: Don't you have any quests for me?

t_ch: 我很想，但贝洛伯格的参赛选手已经有许多了。
t_en: I'd love to, but there are already too many participants in Belobog.

t_ch: ...不过，关于比赛的胜负，我跟佩拉打了个赌。
t_en: ...But here's the thing: I made a bet with Pela about the outcome of the tournament.

t_ch: 如果你真能取得冠军的话，我可以代表个人给你一些奖励。
t_en: If you manage to win the championship, I can give you some rewards on my own behalf.

t_ch: 好，我走了。
t_en: All right. See you then.

t_ch: 我已经如约拿到冠军了。
t_en: I've won the championship as promised.

t_ch: 的确，就连我一开始也没想到你真的能赢...也许这就是无名客的过人之处吧。
t_en: To be honest, I didn't think you would actually win... I guess that's what makes the Nameless so amazing.

t_ch: 拿着，这是给你的奖励。
t_en: Here, the promised reward for you.

t_ch: 好吧，再见。
t_en: All right. See you next time.

t_ch: 再见，记得留心那些黑衣人。
t_en: See you next time. And watch out for those shady guys in black.

t_ch: 好吧，再见。
t_en: All right. See you next time.

t_ch: 再见，记得留心那些黑衣人。
t_en: See you next time. And watch out for those shady guys in black.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{M#先生}{F#小姐}！欢迎来到「星际决胜庆典」会场！
t_en: Hey, {M#Mr.}{F#Ms.} {NICKNAME}! Welcome to the Interastral Tournament Festival!

t_ch: 您想要了解相关信息吗？又或者是前来办理相关手续的？
t_en: Need any information? Or just signing up?

t_ch: 不论您需要什么帮助，都请随时告诉我——身为星际和平公司员工兼《以太战线》资深玩家，我随时有求必应！
t_en: Anything you need, just let me know! As an IPC worker and a veteran player of Aetherium Wars, I'm always ready to help!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{M#先生}{F#小姐}！欢迎来到「星际决胜庆典」会场——相关赛事现在已经结束了。
t_en: Hey, {M#Mr.}{F#Ms.} {NICKNAME}! Welcome to the Interastral Tournament Festival! The tournament is over, though.

t_ch: 不过，您还是可以通过这台综合终端，编辑您的玩家信息和以太灵阵容，与来自全世界的玩家对决——如果您有任何需要就请告诉我吧！
t_en: However, you can use this terminal to edit your information and lineup, and compete against players worldwide. Anything you need, just let me know!

t_ch: 我想和你聊聊…
t_en: Can we talk...?

t_ch: 好啊，您想聊什么？
t_en: Sure thing! What's on your mind?

t_ch: 你很喜欢这款游戏吗？
t_en: Do you like this game?

t_ch: 当然了！要说我和这款游戏的缘分，还得从我的父辈开始讲起呢。
t_en: Oh, absolutely positively so! Let me tell you how I started playing this game! Well, it actually started with my father's generation.

t_ch: 我的父亲是一位电子游戏重度玩家——他从小就带着我和我的双胞胎兄弟，培养我们的游戏兴趣：而这《以太战线》就是我们兄弟俩接触的第一款游戏了。
t_en: My father was a hardcore gamer who introduced my twin brother and me to gaming at a young age, and Aetherium Wars was the first game we ever played.

t_ch: 虽然我在之后也玩了其他不少游戏，其中也不乏一线厂商的各种工业化大制作，但陪伴我最久的反而是这样一款略显简陋的游戏。
t_en: I've tried many other games since then, including blockbusters from top-notch developers, but Aetherium Wars, despite its somewhat dated graphics, has stuck with me the longest.

t_ch: 要说原因…除了这游戏本身足够有趣，又能保证长期更新，我想更多还是因为情怀加成……
t_en: As for why... I think the game itself is fascinating and has consistent updates, but more importantly, it holds sentimental value and that's what makes it special to me...

t_ch: 毕竟，这款游戏是我童年不可或缺的一部分——喜欢它也是很自然的吧？哈哈哈。
t_en: Anyway, this game was a big part of my childhood, and it's only natural to have a soft spot for it, right? Hahaha.

t_ch: 你喜欢这份工作吗？
t_en: Do you enjoy your job?

t_ch: 您是指「星际决胜庆典」的运营吗？那我还是非常乐在其中的。
t_en: You mean working as an operator for the Interastral Tournament Festival? Yeah, I do enjoy it.

t_ch: 为自己热爱的游戏贡献一份属于自己的力量，这恐怕是每个游戏玩家都梦寐以求的工作了——您说对吧？
t_en: Contributing to a game you love is the greatest dream for any gamer. Don't you agree?

t_ch: 虽然日日夜夜站在这里接待各路玩家，我这身体确实有点吃不消…但考虑到这可是《以太战线》，我也就感觉没那么难受了。
t_en: While standing here day and night to greet players does take a toll on my body, but... hey! It's Aetherium Wars! Whenever I think of that, it doesn't feel so bad anymore.

t_ch: 可以聊聊星际和平公司吗？
t_en: Can we talk about IPC?

t_ch: 哈哈，当然没问题！不过总部的消息我也知之甚少，就和您随便聊聊我的工作生活吧。
t_en: Haha, sure thing! I don't know much about the headquarters, so let's just talk about my daily work and life.

t_ch: 我自打从欧文利星毕业以后就拿到了公司「战略投资部」的工作——和我的双胞胎弟弟维利特一起。
t_en: After graduating from Owenley, I got a job with the Strategic Investment Department, where I work together with my twin brother Velite.

t_ch: 到现在为止，我们大概干了有四年…不对，五年了。这期间甚至还跨了一个琥珀纪呢，哈哈哈。
t_en: It's been about four... no, five years since then. Can you believe we've even crossed into a new Amber Era? Hahaha.

t_ch: 我还是挺享受这份工作的：累虽然是累了点，但托帕总监对我们还不错；我那弟弟给另一位总监「翡翠」打工去了，听他每天叫苦不迭，日子貌似挺不好过……
t_en: To be honest, I enjoy my job. While it can be exhausting at times, Director Topaz has been treating me well. My brother is now working for another director named Jade, and he's having a hard time...

t_ch: 先聊到这里吧。
t_en: Let's stop here for now.

t_ch: 祝您玩得开心！
t_en: Have a great time!

t_ch: 我没有需求了，谢谢你。
t_en: I don't need anything else. Thank you.

t_ch: 祝您玩得开心！
t_en: Have a great time!

t_ch: 哼，哼哼？
t_en: Oink, oink oink?

t_ch: 自从决定归还{TEXTJOIN#54}之后，这只扑满的脾性变得更加自由了。即便名义上归属于空间站，它仍会时不时偷溜进列车，仿佛特意寻找你的踪迹。
t_en: Ever since you decided to return {TEXTJOIN#54}, this Trotter's temperament has become even more unrestricted. Even though it technically belongs on the space station, it still occasionally sneaks onto the express, as if specifically it's searching for traces of you.

t_ch: 哼哼...哼，哼哼！
t_en: Oink oink... oink, oink oink!

t_ch: 虽然只是一簇数据，但{TEXTJOIN#54}确实对你表现出了热情，也许它还会梦见电子羊呢。
t_en: Although it's just a bunch of data, {TEXTJOIN#54} really does seem fond of you. Perhaps it even dreams of electric sheep.

t_ch: 哼，哼哼！
t_en: Oink, oink oink!

t_ch: 自从决定归还{TEXTJOIN#54}之后，这只扑满在空间站最大的乐趣就是等待你的到来。你们已经很久没见了——也许吧？
t_en: Ever since you decided to return {TEXTJOIN#54}, the Trotter's greatest joy at the space station has been waiting for your visits. Perhaps, you haven't seen each other in some time?

t_ch: 哼哼...哼，哼哼！
t_en: Oink oink... oink, oink oink!

t_ch: 哼，哼哼？
t_en: Oink, oink oink?

t_ch: 自从决定留下{TEXTJOIN#54}之后，这只扑满的脾性变得更加自由了。它随心所欲地往返于空间站和列车间，仿佛特意追寻你的脚步。
t_en: Ever since you decided to keep {TEXTJOIN#54}, this Trotter's temperament has become even more unrestricted. It roams as it pleases between the space station and Express, as if specifically following your footsteps.

t_ch: 哼哼...哼，哼哼！
t_en: Oink oink... oink, oink oink!

t_ch: 虽然只是一簇数据，但{TEXTJOIN#54}确实对你表现出了热情，也许它还会梦见电子羊呢。
t_en: Although it's just a bunch of data, {TEXTJOIN#54} really does seem fond of you. Perhaps it even dreams of electric sheep.

t_ch: 哼，哼哼！
t_en: Oink, oink oink!

t_ch: 扑满试图引起你的注意，它不愿被忽视。
t_en: The Trotter is trying to get your attention. It does not want to be ignored.

t_ch: 跟它玩一会儿吧。
t_en: Why not play with it a little?

t_ch: 扑满扭动着身子，似乎在等待你的行动。
t_en: The Trotter is wriggling about. It seems to be waiting for you to make a move.

t_ch: （拍拍它的脑袋）
t_en: (Pat it on the head)

t_ch: 哼，哼哼~
t_en: Oink, oink oink~

t_ch: 扑满发出兴奋的声音，时不时拱起鼻子做出回应。它很开心。
t_en: The Trotter lets out a squeal of excitement, scrunching up its nose in response. It's very happy.

t_ch: （捏捏它的鼻子）
t_en: (Squeeze its nose)
Oink... oink oink...

t_ch: 扑满的鼻子格外敏感，有什么东西正在酝酿......
t_en: The Trotter's nose is extremely sensitive. Something seems to be brewing...

t_ch: 哼唧！
t_en: OINK!

t_ch: 一个巨大的喷嚏...幸亏以太灵没有鼻涕。
t_en: It is a gigantic sneeze... Thankfully Aether Spirits don't have snot.

t_ch: （揪揪它的耳朵）
t_en: (Tug on its ear)

t_ch: 哼，哼哼~
t_en: Oink, oink oink~

t_ch: 扑满的逃跑天赋同样反映在它身体的各个部位上。你试图抓住它灵巧的耳朵，手速却怎么都不够用。
t_en: The Trotter's talent for escape is reflected in every part of its body. You try to catch its dexterous ears, but your hand is just not fast enough.

t_ch: 哼，哼哼~
t_en: Oink, oink oink~

t_ch: 听见了吗？那是胜利的哼声。
t_en: Did you hear that? That's the oink of victory.

t_ch: （摸摸它的屁股）
t_en: (Pat its butt)

t_ch: 哼，哼哼！哼——
t_en: Oink, oink oink! Oink...

t_ch: 银河中有句箴言：「扑满的屁股摸不得」，即便它又小又可爱。
t_en: There is a saying across the cosmos: "One must never touch a Trotter's butt," no matter how cute or small it is.

t_ch: 你的举动让扑满很不满，它也该得到尊重。
t_en: The Trotter is very displeased with what you have done. It deserves respect, too.

t_ch: （玩的差不多了）
t_en: (That's enough playing)

t_ch: 扑满看上去还很精神。人不能把有限的精力投入到无限的玩弄扑满中去。
t_en: The Trotter still looks full of energy, but people can't afford to put all of their limited energy into endless play sessions with Trotters.

t_ch: 和扑满说话。
t_en: Speak to the Trotter.

t_ch: 扑满歪了歪头，似乎在等你开口。
t_en: The Trotter tilts its head, as if waiting for you to speak.

t_ch: 猪猪乖~
t_en: Good piggy~

t_ch: 哼，哼哼！哼——
t_en: Oink, oink oink! Oink...

t_ch: 这个称呼让扑满很不满，它不是猪。
t_en: The Trotter does not like this name at all. It is not a pig.

t_ch: 我们是永远的好朋友。
t_en: We're friends forever.

t_ch: 哼~哼哼...
t_en: Oink~ Oink oink...

t_ch: 扑满被你的直球打动，表现得亲密又顺从。
t_en: The Trotter seems touched by your affection. It seems very affectionate and obedient.

t_ch: {TEXTJOIN#54}，使用撞击！
t_en: {TEXTJOIN#54}, charge!
Oink, oink oink...

t_ch: 扑满没有听从命令。可能是它找不到目标，或者单纯在耍个性。
t_en: The Trotter does not listen to your command. Maybe it could not find a target, or maybe it's just showing you some attitude.

t_ch: 回到硬币中吧， {TEXTJOIN#54}！
t_en: Return to your coin, {TEXTJOIN#54}!
Oink, oink oink...

t_ch: 扑满不愿听从这个命令，生命总会找到出路。
t_en: The Trotter does not want to obey this order. Life always finds a way...

t_ch: （先不对猪弹琴了）
t_en: (Give up trying to reason with a pig)

t_ch: 哼，哼哼！
t_en: Oink, oink oink!

t_ch: 扑满期待地看着你，但人不能总是对着动物朋友说话。
t_en: The Trotter is looking at you expectantly, but people can't spend all their time talking to their animal friends.

t_ch: 之后再陪它吧。
t_en: Let's play with it again another time.

t_ch: 哼，哼哼！
t_en: Oink, oink oink!

t_ch: 扑满试图引起你的注意，它好卖力，你好无情。
t_en: The Trotter is trying to get your attention. It's working so hard, and you're cold as ice.
...Arf?

t_ch: 小冰原熊歪着脑袋打量着你，扑扇着两只半圆形的耳朵。
t_en: The little arctic bear cub watches you with its head tilted, flapping its semicircular ears.

t_ch: 一般而言，野生动物对于陌生事物都会抱持警惕的态度——但眼前的小家伙似乎并不介意你的存在。
t_en: Normally, wild animals are alert around unfamiliar things, but this little one doesn't seem to be bothered by your presence.

t_ch: 呜…咕呜！
t_en: Arf... groo!

t_ch: 咕！咕呜呜！
t_en: Arf! Groooo!

t_ch: 小熊发出有些殷切的叫声，挥动起又短又圆的前肢，似乎指望你能帮它做些什么。
t_en: The little bear cub calls out to you eagerly, flapping its short and round limbs. It seems as if it's asking you for help.

t_ch: 谁能拒绝一只可爱的小冰原熊的邀请呢？你走上前……
t_en: Who could reject an invitation from an adorable little arctic bear cub? You start to approach...

t_ch: 你用双手轻轻抱起小熊，它的重量比你想象得要轻一些。随着十指微微嵌入毛发与脂肪交叠的绵软地带，一阵暖意顺着你的掌心蔓延开来。
t_en: You gently pick up the little cub with both hands. It is lighter than you expected. A feeling of warmth spreads across your palms, as your fingers are immersed in the soft area where its fat and fur overlap.

t_ch: 呜！咕呜呜！
t_en: Groo! Groooo!

t_ch: 它发出了开心的叫声。小熊身下原本压着某样东西——或许是它送给你的礼物？
t_en: The cub let out a joyous call. There is something laying underneath where the bear was sat... maybe it's a present for you?

t_ch: 呜咕…呜咕！
t_en: Groo... grooo!

t_ch: 小熊表现得意犹未尽。也许你可以试着表现得更亲密些……
t_en: The cub seems to be in a great mood. Maybe you could try befriending it...
...Arf?

t_ch: 小冰原熊歪着脑袋打量着你，扑扇着两只半圆形的耳朵。
t_en: The little arctic bear cub watches you with its head tilted, flapping its semicircular ears.

t_ch: 呜…咕呜！
t_en: Arf... groo!

t_ch: 咕！咕呜呜！
t_en: Arf! Groooo!

t_ch: 小熊发出有些殷切的叫声，挥动起又短又圆的前肢，似乎希望你能帮它做些什么。
t_en: The little bear cub calls out to you eagerly, flapping its short and round limbs. It seems as if it's asking you for help.

t_ch: 你用双手轻轻抱起小熊，果然——它的身下压着什么东西。
t_en: You lift the bear cub with both hands and as expected... there is something underneath.

t_ch: 呜！咕呜呜！
t_en: Groo! Groooo!

t_ch: 小熊发出了开心的叫声——它压着的东西，或许是原本打算送给你的礼物？
t_en: The cub let out a joyous call... maybe the item underneath it was a present it was planning to give you?

t_en: Groo... grooo!

t_ch: 哎…臭小子总不回信……
t_en: Ah... that no-good kid never wrote me back...

t_ch: 他去的是后勤部，放心吧。
t_en: He went to the Logistics Department. Don't worry.

t_en: Moo-Moo's all-new sour cream!

t_ch: 大守护者精选鱼子酱！

t_ch: 吃出滋养，吃出品位！

t_ch: 居家应急超值之选！
t_en: A super-value choice for family emergencies!

t_en: Caught and swiftly killed to lock in the freshness!

t_en: When will coffee be back on the menu...

t_ch: 不知道啊，我也想喝。
t_en: I don't know. I want a cup too.

t_ch: 好像有小说写过这宾馆呢……
t_en: I think there's a novel written about this hotel...

t_ch: 是啊，可惜故事烂尾了。
t_en: Yeah, a pity that it never got finished.

t_ch: 叮叮号好像有段恐怖传说……
t_en: I heard there's a legend of horror about the Ding-Ding Tram...

t_ch: 你是说「不存在的车站」吧？
t_en: You mean the one about the station that doesn't exist?

t_ch: 电话亭好像有段恐怖传说……
t_en: I heard there's a legend of horror about the phone booth...

t_ch: …谁半夜三点打电话啊？
t_en: ...Who calls at 3 AM?

t_ch: 你品品这能量菌菇煎茶……

t_ch: 这不就一股苦味儿吗？
t_en: Isn't that just bitter?

t_ch: 你品品这蜂蜜燕麦慕斯……
t_en: Have a taste of this Honey and Oats Mousse...

t_ch: 哎呦喂，腻死我了……
t_en: Ack, I'm sick of that...

t_en: I don't know what flowers she likes...

t_ch: 给妈妈买什么好呢？
t_en: What should I buy for my mom?

t_ch: 初雪八落吧，送老人准没错。
t_en: First Snow. They're great for oldies.

t_ch: 哎，你快出出主意。
t_en: Hey, hurry up and give me some ideas.

t_ch: 不是，我也不懂花啊……
t_en: No, I don't understand flowers either...

t_ch: 就买暖阳花吧！
t_en: Just buy some Sunshine!

t_ch: 唔…我觉得覆夏竹更好。
t_en: Hmm... I think Summershade Bamboo is better.

t_ch: 嗯，这个好。
t_en: Mmm, this is good.

t_ch: 我们就给妈妈买这个吧！
t_en: Let's buy mom these ones!

t_ch: 这天气喝茶，就该坐屋里……
t_en: Drinking tea in this weather should be done indoors...

t_ch: 城里不都一个天气？
t_en: Isn't the weather the same across the city?

t_ch: 这天气喝茶，就该坐外边……
t_en: Drinking tea in this weather should be done outdoors...

t_ch: 城里不都一个天气？
t_en: Isn't the weather the same across the city?

t_ch: 你那课题怎么样？
t_en: What about that research?

t_ch: 嗐，快别提了……
t_en: Ack, don't remind me...

t_ch: 我想再点个红肠拼盘……
t_en: I think I'll order another sausage platter...

t_ch: 大哥，算了算了。
